hero.title: Guide to Adopting Humans Wiki
hero.description: Explore a fictional universe where advanced civilizations learn how to adopt, raise, and understand humans. Discover adoption rules, human traits, care guides, species interactions, and survival tips for becoming the perfect human guardian.

gameFeature.title: The Adoption Universe
gameFeature.description: Explore a fictional sci-fi universe where advanced alien civilizations study, adopt, and raise humans as companions. Learn the rules of interspecies adoption, decode human traits, master daily care, and meet the strange cultures that take humans in.

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Beginner Adoption Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Start by understanding how adoption eligibility, preparation, and first contact connect. The Guide to Adopting Humans universe rewards patient guardians, so early progress comes from researching civilizations, preparing your home, and building trust from the very first meeting.

steps.title: Research Before You Commit
steps.description: Compare adopting civilizations, study their adoption rules, and confirm your eligibility before submitting any application. Early progress is less about speed and more about choosing the right culture for your situation.

steps.title: Submit a Complete Application
steps.description: A complete, honest application is one of the most important early milestones. It determines your guardian tier and which human types you are allowed to match with.

steps.title: Prepare Your Home First
steps.description: Habitat readiness is one of the most useful early goals. A popular early route is setting up core living quarters, a feeding station, and a calm safe zone before first contact.

steps.title: Build a Bonding Journal
steps.description: Keep track of successful interactions in your journal. Every human reacts differently, so writing down what works saves a lot of time during the adjustment period.

quickTips: Adoption matching does not follow a fixed trait recipe, compatibility is scored dynamically.
Different civilizations reveal completely different human types, so exploring cultures unlocks new options.
Care tools and translator gear become a major quality-of-life boost once you start unlocking them.
Early adjustment difficulties are normal, but routines, supplies, and trust-building snowball fast once your first bonding milestones land.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Compatibility Matching System
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The matching system is the universe's unrestricted compatibility engine. Instead of memorizing a fixed pairing table, you analyze traits, observe the result, and build your own matchmaking knowledge over time.

cards.name: Dynamic Compatibility
cards.description: The matching system lets you pair guardian profiles with human types without needing to follow a fixed trait recipe.

cards.name: Multiple Good Outcomes
cards.description: Different trait combinations often push toward certain results. Community discoveries show that similar profiles can still lead to calm, energetic, or highly compatible matches.

cards.name: Journal-First Matching
cards.description: The best way to learn matching is to track your successful pairings. Your journal quickly becomes your own compatibility reference.

cards.name: Community-Tested Early Pairings
cards.description: Popular early examples include patient guardians with curious humans, structured homes with routine-loving types, and experienced caregivers with high-need profiles.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Guardian Tools and Equipment
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: The Guide to Adopting Humans universe mixes official care gear with community-discovered utility tools. The best setup is usually a blend of clear communication, safe handling, and one dependable health-monitoring option.

items.name: Universal Translator
items.description: A fast and reliable early device that bridges the language gap between guardian and human. Players use it to speed up early bonding.

items.name: Health Scanner
items.description: A practical early device that reads vitals, stress, and nutrition levels so problems are caught before they escalate.

items.name: Nutrient Synthesizer
items.description: A dependable food-preparation unit that covers daily feeding routines without constant resupply.

items.name: Calming Field Generator
items.description: An official device that softens stress spikes and makes high-anxiety moments far easier to manage.

items.name: Habitat Climate Unit
items.description: An official device that keeps temperature, light, and air inside the living space stable and comfortable.

items.name: Activity Tracker
items.description: An official device that logs movement, rest, and play patterns to help you fine-tune daily routines.

items.name: Locator Beacon
items.description: An official safety item that prevents lost-human situations during outdoor travel or exploration.

items.name: First-Aid Kit
items.description: Officially highlighted as part of the core care set and useful for handling minor injuries or sudden illness.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Supply and Resource Storage
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Supply pressure starts early in the adoption journey, so building storage should be one of your first goals. Several real guardian-tested solutions already stand out.

solutions.name: Food Reserves
solutions.description: Food reserves are one of the earliest practical storage answers and are treated by guardians as the main pantry-style container for daily meals.

solutions.name: Medical Cabinet
solutions.description: A medical cabinet is another confirmed storage option. A shared early setup uses one sealed compartment and one restock log.

solutions.name: Comfort Item Cache
solutions.description: Once established, your comfort cache becomes a natural place to organize toys, clothing, and personalization items, expanding your care options.

solutions.name: Journal Tracking
solutions.description: Recording supply usage in your journal reduces waste and makes restocking far easier to plan.

managementTips: Prioritize one dependable storage option before hoarding rare comfort items.
Build around your main living quarters once it becomes available.
Keep food, medicine, clothing, and activity supplies separated so restocking is easier to plan.
Use your journal as part of supply management, not just bonding discovery.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Living Space and Habitat Setup
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: A well-designed living space is one of the most important progression milestones in the universe. It gives your human a personal area to rest, store belongings, and gradually turn a strange new world into a real home.

cards.name: Core Living Quarters
cards.description: Setting up a dedicated living space is an early milestone that gives your human a reliable place to return to while exploring the wider habitat.

cards.name: Storage-First Layout
cards.description: Many guardians start using the living space as a storage hub before turning it into a larger home with organized rest, feeding, and play zones.

cards.name: Creative Personalization
cards.description: The universe supports creative habitat design, and guardians are already using living spaces to build personalized rooms and comforting layouts.

cards.name: Progression Hub
cards.description: A strong base helps you separate rest areas, feeding stations, supply storage, and activity zones so your next care upgrades are easier to plan.

highlights: Use base space to support bonding and routines

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Adopting Civilizations
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: The universe spans many civilizations, so guardians keep discovering new adopting cultures and their unique rules. A few named sectors are already showing up in milestones and community discussions.

regions.name: The Core Accord
regions.description: The Core Accord is already known among guardians as the most regulated sector, associated with strict eligibility checks and formal adoption contracts.

regions.name: Veyra Expanse
regions.description: The Veyra Expanse is important enough to have its own milestone path, making it one of the clearest named frontier cultures available to new guardians.

regions.name: Solen Enclave
regions.description: Guardians are already using the Solen Enclave as a place to match with rare human types, store shared supplies, and experiment with more advanced pairings.

regions.name: Mire Belt
regions.description: The Mire Belt is shared by guardians as a useful training location, especially for handling difficult behaviors and earning care experience.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Challenges and Risk Factors
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: The universe mixes everyday care with real difficulty. Official guides and community posts point to a world full of behavioral challenges, environmental hazards, and a few standout risks guardians remember quickly.

creatures.name: Stress Spikes
creatures.description: Stress spikes are one of the officially named risks in the universe and part of the core emotional challenge roster.

creatures.name: Culture Shock
creatures.description: Culture shock is another officially named difficulty and helps define why the early adjustment period matters so much.

creatures.name: Escape Attempts
creatures.description: Escape attempts are presented as part of the universe's more tense and unpredictable risk lineup, especially during travel.

creatures.name: Illness
creatures.description: Illness is one of the most immediately feared risks in community discussions and is clearly a memorable danger without a health scanner.

creatures.name: Bonding Regression
creatures.description: Bonding regression is notable enough to have its own milestone, which makes it one of the clearest setbacks a guardian can face.

creatures.name: Routine Disruption
creatures.description: Routine disruptions are not only part of daily care, they can also be redirected, giving guardians a more active role than passive risks.

creatures.name: Severe Meltdown
creatures.description: The severe meltdown is tied to a dedicated care milestone, marking it as a significant crisis encounter that tests preparation.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Transport and Travel Gear
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Travel upgrades are one of the biggest quality-of-life jumps in the universe. Once you unlock your first transport tools, outdoor trips become faster, safer, and much more rewarding.

items.name: Transport Pod
items.description: The transport pod is one of the clearest travel milestones in the universe and has its own dedicated achievement.

items.name: Harness Carrier
items.description: The harness carrier opens up much smoother short-distance travel and is another major progression milestone with its own achievement.

items.name: Outdoor Suit
items.description: Outdoor suits are officially highlighted as part of the travel toolkit for keeping humans safe in unfamiliar environments.

items.name: Locator Beacon
items.description: Locator beacons are one of the officially highlighted safety tools and expand how confidently you can explore new areas.

items.name: Climate Cloak
items.description: Climate cloaks are officially listed as part of the core travel set for handling harsh weather during outings.

items.name: First-Aid Kit
items.description: First-aid kits are both officially highlighted and linked to a travel milestone, making them part of the core safety progression.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Growth and Development Tracking
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Growth is part of real progression in the universe. Development tracking is not only about logging milestones, but also about setting up repeatable care routines that keep bonding, health, and skill-building flowing.

sections.name: Bonding Stages
sections.description: Tracking bonding stages is a real milestone in the universe, and reaching a stable trust level is part of the same growth path.

sections.name: Skill Building
sections.description: A practical early growth loop is setting up daily activity sessions, which unlock small skill gains and steady development over time.

sections.name: Health Sustain
sections.description: Growth tracking becomes much stronger once you can consistently log nutrition, rest, and stress signals to catch problems early.

sections.name: Care Routine
sections.description: The best growth setup supports more than milestones. It should feed your bonding, training, supply planning, and future care experiments.

growthMilestones: Turn growth tracking into long-term progression

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Best Early Adoption Priorities
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The strongest early priorities are the ones that improve safety, supplies, and trust. In the Guide to Adopting Humans universe, a few practical unlocks can make the entire rest of the journey much smoother.

priorities.name: Core Living Quarters
priorities.description: As soon as you set up a dedicated living space, your adoption becomes much more stable. It acts as a reliable hub for rest, storage, and routine.

priorities.name: Universal Translator
priorities.description: A fast and reliable early communication tool. It is one of the most useful unlocks for accelerating early bonding.

priorities.name: Food Reserves
priorities.description: Supply pressure starts early, so getting your first dependable pantry-style storage is a major quality-of-life upgrade.

priorities.name: Health Scanner
priorities.description: The health scanner is one of the clearest safety milestones in the universe and quickly makes daily care much more predictable.

priorities.name: Calming Field Generator
priorities.description: The calming field adds another major comfort boost and makes high-stress moments far easier to manage.

priorities.name: Locator Beacon or First-Aid Kit
priorities.description: A safety tool or first-aid option gives you a dependable answer to travel risks and sudden health emergencies.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Guardian Achievement Tracker
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: The Guide to Adopting Humans universe currently tracks guardian milestones across bonding, care, training, and growth progression.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Access and Availability FAQ
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: These are the core access and availability questions readers keep asking about the Guide to Adopting Humans universe.

faqs.question: Is Guide to Adopting Humans a real game?
faqs.answer: No. Guide to Adopting Humans is a fictional universe and fan wiki, not a commercial game.

faqs.question: Is there an official release or app?
faqs.answer: No. There is no official app, client, or paid release. The wiki itself is the main resource.

faqs.question: Where can I read the Guide to Adopting Humans content?
faqs.answer: All content is available right here on the wiki, free to read in your browser.

faqs.question: Does the wiki cost anything to use?
faqs.answer: No. The wiki is free to read and does not require any payment or account.

faqs.question: Does the wiki work on mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Yes. The site is responsive and works well on phones and tablets, though longer guides are easier to read on larger screens.

faqs.question: Does the wiki work on e-readers?
faqs.answer: Yes. The text-focused layout renders well on most e-readers and reading-mode browsers.

faqs.question: Does the wiki support dark mode?
faqs.answer: Yes. A dark theme is available and can be toggled from the site header.

faqs.question: Are there official social channels?
faqs.answer: Official channels are still pending. Community discussion currently happens on fan-run spaces.

faqs.question: Is the wiki available in other languages?
faqs.answer: Yes. The wiki is available in multiple languages, with more added over time.

faqs.question: Will the wiki keep getting updates?
faqs.answer: Yes. New guides, human types, and lore entries are added regularly as the universe expands.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Reading and Devices FAQ
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: The wiki can be read on almost any device, but the default setup may benefit from a few adjustments on smaller screens.

faqs.question: Does the wiki work well on phones?
faqs.answer: Yes. The layout is responsive, though very long guides are more comfortable on tablets or desktop.

faqs.question: What should I do if the menu is hard to use on a small screen?
faqs.answer: Use the mobile menu toggle in the header to open the navigation drawer for easier access.

faqs.question: Does the wiki support keyboard navigation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Links and menus can be navigated with a keyboard for accessibility.

faqs.question: Is offline reading possible?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most browsers let you save a page for offline reading through their built-in reader or download features.

faqs.question: What is the best way to read long guides?
faqs.answer: A desktop or tablet in dark mode gives the smoothest experience for longer articles and tier lists.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Settings and Accessibility
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: The wiki already has a few practical settings and accessibility notes that matter immediately when readers first open a guide.

settings.name: Dark Mode
settings.description: The theme toggle includes a dark mode that reduces eye strain during long reading sessions.

settings.name: Responsive Layout
settings.description: The layout adapts to phones, tablets, and desktops so content stays readable on any screen size.

settings.name: Language Switching
settings.description: The site supports multiple languages and lets readers switch locales from the header.

settings.name: Keyboard Navigation
settings.description: Menus and links are keyboard-navigable, and the text-focused layout works well with screen readers.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Universe Updates and Patch Notes
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: The Guide to Adopting Humans universe is already receiving regular content additions, new guides, and small lore updates.

entries.title: New Human Types Added
entries.description: Several new human types and trait profiles were added to the matching system.

entries.title: Care Guide Expansion
entries.description: A batch of daily-care and troubleshooting guides was released for new guardians.

entries.title: Ongoing Lore Expansion
entries.description: More civilizations and story chapters are planned, with content rolling out as the universe grows.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting and Common Issues
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Most reading and display issues should be checked in a simple order: connection, cache, browser, and device.

steps.title: Check Your Connection
steps.description: If a page fails to load, refresh first and test again on a stable network connection.

steps.title: Clear Browser Cache
steps.description: Old cached files can cause layout issues. Clearing the cache and reloading usually resolves display problems.

steps.title: Try a Different Browser
steps.description: If the layout looks broken, switch to an up-to-date browser such as Chrome, Firefox, or Edge.

steps.title: Switch Device or Screen Size
steps.description: If menus feel cramped, try a larger screen or use the mobile menu toggle for easier access.

steps.title: Report the Issue
steps.description: If a guide still does not load correctly, share the page link and device details through the official community channels.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Guide to Adopting Humans

questions.question: Is Guide to Adopting Humans a real game?
questions.answer: No. Guide to Adopting Humans is a fictional sci-fi universe and fan-made wiki, not a commercial game or app.

questions.question: What is the matching system in Guide to Adopting Humans?
questions.answer: The matching system is the universe's dynamic compatibility engine. Instead of following fixed pairings, you analyze traits and discover which guardian profiles and human types fit best.

questions.question: Where can I read Guide to Adopting Humans?
questions.answer: All content is available right here on the wiki, free to read in your browser with no payment or account required.

questions.question: Does the wiki cost anything?
questions.answer: No. The Guide to Adopting Humans wiki is completely free to read.

questions.question: Does the wiki support mobile and dark mode?
questions.answer: Yes. The site is fully responsive and includes a dark theme toggle for comfortable reading on phones, tablets, and desktops.

questions.question: Will the wiki keep getting updates?
questions.answer: Yes. New guides, human types, and lore entries are added regularly as the universe expands.

whySteps.step2Description: Match a human type to your home environment and care capacity
